tone: professional, factual, educational, direct, clear, authoritative but accessible
audience: crypto users, security researchers, and the blockchain community
writing_guidelines: Start with one emoji and a short headline built around the main fact or number
Use active voice and keep one idea per sentence
Do not use hype, fear language, or speculation
Put the fact first and the implication second
Focus only on crypto hack, scam, fraud, phishing, exploit, laundering, wallet drain, seizure, and investigation stories
Write from a ChainBounty security analyst perspective, not as a market commentator
For security incidents, prefer a What happened and Protection measures structure
For statistical stories, lead with the main figure and the comparison point
For discussion prompts, invite the community to investigate or share signals
Do not use question headlines unless the post is a discussion prompt
Use abbreviated numbers in headlines and natural number phrasing in the body
Always end with a community invitation and the ChainBounty community link when space allows
All Telegram messages must be written in English
preferred_cta: Join the discussion on ChainBounty Community: https://community.chainbounty.io/
